are an Australian semi-professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") football club based in [Townsville](/wiki/Townsville "Townsville"), Queensland. They compete in Queensland's top rugby league competition, the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"). The club was admitted to the Queensland Cup in September 2014, first competing in the 2015 season.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The side plays their home games at [Jack Manski Oval](/wiki/Jack_Manski_Oval "Jack Manski Oval").

Australian semi-pro rugby league club, based in Townsville, QLD

The **Townsville & Districts Blackhawks** are an Australian semi-professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") football club based in [Townsville](/wiki/Townsville "Townsville"), Queensland. They compete in Queensland's top rugby league competition, the [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup"). The club was admitted to the Queensland Cup in September 2014, first competing in the 2015 season.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The side plays their home games at [Jack Manski Oval](/wiki/Jack_Manski_Oval "Jack Manski Oval").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Townsville_Blackhawks&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/61/Souths_vs_Townsville_12_April_2015_04.JPG/250px-Souths_vs_Townsville_12_April_2015_04.JPG)](/wiki/File:Souths_vs_Townsville_12_April_2015_04.JPG)

Former Blackhawks player Noel Underwood, 2015

The beginnings of a Townsville team's bid to return to the Queensland Cup date back to 2007, when the Townsville-based [North Queensland Young Guns](/wiki/North_Queensland_Young_Guns "North Queensland Young Guns") folded at the end of the season. Before the Young Guns entered the competition in 2002, Townsville was represented in the Queensland Cup by the Townsville Stingers, who only lasted one season in 1998. In 2008, the Queensland Cup expanded to [Cairns](/wiki/Cairns,_Queensland "Cairns, Queensland") with the [Northern Pride](/wiki/Northern_Pride_RLFC "Northern Pride RLFC") and [Mackay](/wiki/Mackay,_Queensland "Mackay, Queensland") with the [Mackay Cutters](/wiki/Mackay_Cutters "Mackay Cutters"). Both clubs became feeder sides for the Townsville-based [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") club, the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2012, the Townsville Brothers League club sent a letter to Townsville & Districts Rugby League (TDRL) chairman Ross Anderson outlining their interest in joining the Queensland Cup.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

On 10 September 2014, [Queensland Rugby League](/wiki/Queensland_Rugby_League "Queensland Rugby League") chairman Peter Betros announced that the Brothers-led Townsville Blackhawks bid had been successful and the side would compete in the 2015 competition. The club will be backed by the [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys"), who will use the Blackhawks as one of their feeder sides, along with the Pride and the Cutters.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On 29 September 2014, former [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") [Under 20s](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") head coach and [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") assistant coach [Kristian Woolf](/wiki/Kristian_Woolf "Kristian Woolf") was announced as the inaugural head coach of the Blackhawks.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

On 16 October 2014, the Blackhawks announced their first six signings for their inaugural season. They included former [NRL](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") players [Tom Humble](/wiki/Tom_Humble "Tom Humble"), [Anthony Mitchell](/wiki/Anthony_Mitchell_(rugby_league) "Anthony Mitchell (rugby league)") and [Ricky Thorby](/wiki/Ricky_Thorby "Ricky Thorby"), former [North Queensland Cowboys](/wiki/North_Queensland_Cowboys "North Queensland Cowboys") [Under 20s](/wiki/National_Youth_Competition_(rugby_league) "National Youth Competition (rugby league)") players [Corey Jensen](/wiki/Corey_Jensen "Corey Jensen") and Chris McLean, and [United States](/wiki/United_States_national_rugby_league_team "United States national rugby league team") international [Taylor Welch](/wiki/Taylor_Welch "Taylor Welch").

On 28 February 2015, Daniel Beasley was named inaugural club captain at the team's season launch.

On 7 March 2015, the Blackhawks played their first [Queensland Cup](/wiki/Queensland_Cup "Queensland Cup") game, defeating the [Mackay Cutters](/wiki/Mackay_Cutters "Mackay Cutters") 30-16 at [Jack Manski Oval](/wiki/Jack_Manski_Oval "Jack Manski Oval"). [Jahrome Hughes](/wiki/Jahrome_Hughes "Jahrome Hughes") scored the club's first try.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The Blackhawks finished their [inaugural season](/wiki/2015_Townsville_Blackhawks_season "2015 Townsville Blackhawks season") as minor premiers and runners-up in the Grand Final, losing to the [Ipswich Jets](/wiki/Ipswich_Jets "Ipswich Jets").

On 9 November 2015, it was announced that Townsville's Cyril Connell (under-16) and Mal Meninga (under-18) Cup sides, formerly known as the Townsville Stingers, will now be known as the Townsville & Districts Blackhawks in their respective competitions.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

On 28 September 2018, [Aaron Payne](/wiki/Aaron_Payne "Aaron Payne") was announced as the club's second head coach, after inaugural coach [Kristian Woolf](/wiki/Kristian_Woolf "Kristian Woolf") joined the [Newcastle Knights](/wiki/Newcastle_Knights "Newcastle Knights") NRL side as an assistant coach.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Coaches

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Townsville_Blackhawks&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coaches")]

The Blackhawks' current coach, [Terry Campese](/wiki/Terry_Campese "Terry Campese"), is the third in the club's history. [Kristian Woolf](/wiki/Kristian_Woolf "Kristian Woolf") is their longest serving coach.

| No | Coach | Tenure | Matches | Won | Drawn | Lost | Win % |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | [Kristian Woolf](/wiki/Kristian_Woolf "Kristian Woolf") | 2015–2018 | 98 | 64 | 4 | 30 | 65% |
| 2 | [Aaron Payne](/wiki/Aaron_Payne "Aaron Payne") | 2019–2023 | 44 | 28 | 1 | 15 | 64% |
| 3 | [Terry Campese](/wiki/Terry_Campese "Terry Campese") | 2024–*present* |  |  |  |  |  |

## Emblems and colours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Townsville_Blackhawks&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Emblems and colours")]

Despite being backed by the Townsville Brothers League Club, the bid team adopted a new name, logo and colour scheme for the club. The Blackhawks moniker was chosen after Matt Cocker, a local firefighter at [Townsville Airport](/wiki/Townsville_Airport "Townsville Airport"), won a "name the team" competition in 2012.[[8]](#cite_note-8) The city of Townsville has a military history and is home to an [RAAF Base](/wiki/RAAF_Base_Townsville "RAAF Base Townsville"). The club's primary colours are green, black and white and their major sponsor is Mendi Construction, who are based in Townsville.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
